data.title: Stock index futures largely unmoved as investors eye inflation data
data.description:
Stock index futures were little changed on Thursday, with September's consumer inflation data slated to come later.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.08%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.03%.

Major U.S. stock averages closed in the green on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Dow (DJI) ending at new peaks after the Federal Reserve's September meeting minutes said a "substantial majority" on its rate-setting board backed its big half-point cut to start an easing cycle.

"Markets have put in another strong performance over the last 24 hours, as optimism about the near-term economic outlook continues to build," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"Reading the (FOMC) Minutes, CPI is important to what happens next," Economist Steven Blitz from Global Data TS Lombard said.

Investors will now turn their attention to the CPI print, to garner fresh clues on the Fed's monetary policy.

The September CPI data will come before the bell, and is expected to rise 2.3% on a yearly basis, and 0.1% on a monthly basis.

Core CPI is forecasted to rise 0.2% month over month, and 3.2% year-on-year.

The CPI report is in particular focus after the strong jobs report last Friday, as there’s been growing speculation that the Fed might not cut at all at their next meeting in November, Allen added.

The initial jobless claims will also land at the same time, and is expected to rise to 231K.

The Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

data.title: Gritstone bio files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, shares tumble
data.description:
Gritstone bio (NASDAQ:GRTS) has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in Delaware, according to a court filing.

The California-based biotech intends to use the court-administered restructuring process to preserve value and support its ongoing strategic alternatives process. The company is in talks with a party to act as a stalking horse bidder, with plans to present an agreement to the court as early as next week.

During its financial restructuring process, the company intends to operate in the ordinary course and remains committed to advancing its clinical programs, including its ongoing neoantigen immunotherapy and infectious disease programs.

The firm has estimated liabilities between $100M - $500M and assets between $100M - $500M.

Shares of the cash-strapped biotechnology firm fell 35% premarket on Thursday and are down over 90% YTD.

Earlier this month, Gritstone bio (GRTS) stock plunged after the cancer vaccine developer reported data for its GRANITE immunotherapy and said it had retained a financial advisor to review strategic options.

Interim Phase 2 data for GRANITE in the treatment of metastatic microsatellite stable colorectal cancer showed a 21% relative risk reduction of progression or death vs. control in all treated populations.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: GXO, TXG, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were lower while Treasury yields rose on Thursday ahead of inflation data due later in the day.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Shares of GXO Logistics (GXO) surged 6% as the company, a supply chain services provider spun off from XPO Inc. in 2021, is reportedly exploring a potential sale after receiving interest from potential buyers, according to sources familiar with the matter. The Greenwich, Connecticut-based company, with a market value of around $6B, is said to be working with financial advisers to evaluate acquisition offers, including interest from rival logistics providers. While no final decision has been made and the outcome remains uncertain, the discussions are being kept confidential.

Biggest stock losers

  • Shares of E2open Parent Holdings (ETWO), a supply chain software company, plunged 26% following the release of its mixed FQ2 results. The company's adjusted revenue fell short of analysts' expectations, primarily due to delays in finalizing some significant deals. These delays were due to extended client decision timelines. In light of these developments, the company revised its revenue forecast for FY2025 downward, projecting a range of $607M to $617M. This revised outlook is below the consensus estimate of $627.51M.
  • 10x Genomics (TXG) shares plummeted over 26% after the life-science technology company announced preliminary Q3 revenue of approximately $151.7M, a 1% decline from the same period last year and below the consensus of $162.26M. The company attributed the shortfall to modifications in its commercial processes and organization, as well as cautious consumer spending habits.

data.title: Ares Management prices $750M senior notes
data.description:
• Ares Management (NYSE:ARES) priced an offering of $750M aggregate principal amount of its 5.600% senior notes due 2054.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 11, 2024.
  • The net proceeds will be approximately $737.7M.
  • Net proceeds will be used for the payment of a portion of the cash consideration due in acquisition of the international business of GLP Capital and general corporate purposes. The company may choose to invest pending net proceeds in short-term investments.

data.title: Meta sees proceedings ended by German cartel office - report
data.description:
• German cartel authorities ended proceedings against Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) over how personal data is used after the company agreed to take measures addressing their concerns, Reuters reported.
  • Andreas Mundt Cartel Office President said that the key point was that is that using Facebook no longer requires consent to the unlimited collection and allocation of data to one's own user account, even if the data does not actually arise in the Facebook service, the report added.

data.title: GXO Logistics jumps 6% on takeover interest - report
data.description:
GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) stock price gained 6% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading on reports of potential sale.

The company is working with financial advisers and reviewing its options after receiving interest from potential buyers, according to a Bloomberg report that cites people with knowledge of the matter.

The logistics provider (GXO) spun off from trucking company XPO (XPO) in 2021 and has a market cap of almost $6 billion. GXO (GXO) lost about 22% of its market value since its spinoff from parent XPO (XPO), the report said.

Year-to-date, its stock price lost ~17% amid sluggish demand in the UK and Europe last year.

GXO Logistics (GXO) providers of supply chain management tools including AI-powered robotics, warehousing and distribution, order fulfillment, e-commerce, reverse logistics, and other supply chain services for various industries including aerospace & defense.

The logistics provider saw a 19% rise in its revenue during Q2. The company gets a Hold rating on Seeking Alpha, with the lowest factor grades given to momentum.

data.title: E2open Parent Holdings down 24% on lowered FY25 guidance
data.description:
• E2open Parent Holdings press release (NYSE:ETWO): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05 in-line.
  • Revenue of $152.19M (-4.0% Y/Y) misses by $5.68M.
  • GAAP subscription revenue for the second quarter of 2025 was $131.6 million, a decrease of 2.3% from the year-ago comparable period and 86.5% of total revenue.

  • Fiscal 2025 and Fiscal Third Quarter GAAP Subscription Revenue

    • GAAP subscription revenue for fiscal 2025 is expected to be in the range of $526 million to $532 million, reflecting a negative 1.5% organic growth rate at the mid-point. The company had announced prior guidance of $532M-$542M during Q1 results.
    • GAAP subscription revenue for the fiscal third quarter of 2025 is expected to be in the range of $130 million to $133 million, reflecting a negative 1.0% organic growth rate at the mid-point.

  • Fiscal 2025 Guidance

    • Total GAAP revenue for fiscal 2025 is expected to be in the range of $607 million to $617 million, reflecting a negative 3.6% organic growth rate at the mid-point.
    • Non-GAAP gross profit margin for fiscal 2025 is expected to be in the range of 68% to 70%.
    • Adjusted EBITDA for fiscal 2025 is expected to be at the low end of the previously provided range of $215 million to $225 million with an implied adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 35%.

data.title: Japan's Seven & i to set up holding company for non-core assets amid Couche-Tard bid
data.description:
Japan's Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDY) on Thursday unveiled plans to establish an intermediate holding company that will preside over the retailer's supermarket food business, specialty store and other businesses, and is planning to change its name.

The intermediate company will be tasked "with a major role of planning the corporate strategies, managing, and supporting" the non-core business, whose growth story differs from the convenience store business’s, the owner of 7-Eleven brand said in a release.

The initiative comes as Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (OTCPK:ANCTF) reportedly raised its acquisition offer for Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDY) to over $47 billion. The new proposal at $18.19 per share is more than 20% higher than Couche-Tard's (TSX:ATD:CA) previously rejected offer for the 7-Eleven owner sent last month.

Seven & i Holdings (OTCPK:SVNDY), which operates over 80,000 worldwide, has been pressured by investors to divest from its large portfolio of non-core businesses.

The holding company is expected to entail a total of 31 establishments, including the group's superstores business, general goods store Loft, baby goods store Akachan Honpo.

Seven & i will also be named York Holdings, to become an equity method affiliate by February 2025, with an initial public offering planned.

Shares of Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDY) eased 0.43% on the Japanese stock exchange on Thursday, after ending 4.7% higher in the previous session.

data.title: European markets mixed ahead of U.S. inflation numbers
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.07%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.33% Retail sales in Germany increased 1.6% M/M in August.

France (CAC:IND) -0.31% following modest gains in the previous session.

The annual consumer inflation rate in Norway rose to 3% in September.

The annual inflation in Denmark inched lower to 1.3% in September.

Industrial production in Austria dropped by 2.5% Y/Y in August.

The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic rose to 2.6% in September.

Industrial production in Slovakia rose by 0.9% Y/Y in August.

Industrial production in Sweden dropped by 3.2% Y/Y in August. Household spending in Sweden rose by 0.8% M/M in August. The Swedish economy expanded 1.1% M/M in August. Household spending in Sweden rose by 0.8% M/M in August.

The annual inflation rate in Hungary eased to 3% in September. The annual inflation rate in Hungary eased to 3% in September.

Industrial production in Turkey slipped by 5.3% Y/Y in August. The seasonally adjusted jobless rate in Turkey fell to 8.5% in August.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.09% lower, as traders were cautious ahead of the highly-anticipated US CPI report due later in the day. The data should offer further insights on how price pressures in the US economy are evolving, after the FOMC minutes reinforced the view the Fed will lower borrowing costs at a slower pace than initially expected.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the European front.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis points to 4.08%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.28%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.21%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)
